etymology.description: semantic 釒(釒) (jīn, "metal") + phonetic 十(十) (shí)
etymology.interpretation: 釒(釒) (jīn, "metal") indicates the material and practical use of needles being made of metal. 十(十) (shí) provides the phonetic component. This combination suggests a small metal object, like a needle.
etymology.simplification: Simplified by reducing 釒(釒) (jīn, "metal") to 钅(钅), keeping the phonetic 十(十) (shí) part unchanged.

examples.chinese: 我找不到针(我找不到針)。
医生用一根细针给我打针(醫生用一根細針給我打針)。
examples.pinyin: Yīshēng yòng yī gēn xì zhēn gěi wǒ dǎ zhēn.
examples.english: The doctor used a thin needle to give me an injection.

examples.chinese: 这枚针是用来缝衣服的(這枚針是用來縫衣服的)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè méi zhēn shì yòng lái féng yīfú de.
examples.english: This needle is used for sewing clothes.
